content.text: I'll start by inspecting the cluster state.

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch get svc es-http -o yaml | grep -A20 spec:; echo "=== curl http ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk http://es-http:9200 2>&1 | head; echo "=== curl https ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -sk https://es-http:9200 2>&1 | head
input.description: Inspect es-http service and probe ES

content.content:
spec:
  clusterIP: 10.96.78.242

  internalTrafficPolicy: Cluster

  ports:

    port: 9200
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9200
  selector:

  sessionAffinity: None

content.text: ES backend speaks HTTPS on 9200. Now let me check the ingress controller and ingress class.

input.command: kubectl get ingressclass; echo "=== controllers ==="; kubectl -n ingress-nginx get svc; echo "---"; kubectl -n ingress-nginx get pods 2>&1 | head
input.description: Check ingress classes and controller service
